_comment: Fixture for #192 acceptance criterion #1: prove the five routing classes (patch, minor, major, 0x-minor, prerelease) plus the patch-sensitive override and three edge cases (downgrade, same-version, unparseable). Each sample is a synthetic (current, target, depMeta) triple fed to classifyUpgrade(). These are NOT real AIRP dependencies — they are deliberately minimal cases that exercise the classifier. Real upgrade detection against upstream registries is a documented future step (see README.md 'Deferred').

samples.description: Patch bump on a major>=1, non-sensitive dep. Auto-PR allowed.
samples.name: serde

samples.description: Patch bump on a major>=1 dep whose name matches a sensitive pattern ('tokio'). Escalated to patch-sensitive -> issue.
samples.name: tokio

samples.description: Patch bump on a major>=1 dep with area tag 'cryptography'. Escalated to patch-sensitive -> issue, even though the name doesn't match a pattern.
samples.name: fast-hasher

samples.description: Minor bump on a major>=1 dep (3.4 -> 3.5). Issue required.
samples.name: vue

samples.description: 0.x dep patch bump (0.3.49 -> 0.3.50). Treated as major risk per DEV-GUIDE §7.1. Issue required. (The sensitive-name override does NOT fire here because the override only applies to major>=1 patch bumps; 0x-minor is already issue.)
samples.name: time

samples.description: 0.x dep minor bump (0.7 -> 0.8). Treated as major risk per DEV-GUIDE §7.1. Issue required.
samples.name: axum

samples.description: Target is a beta prerelease. Never auto-adopted. Skipped.
samples.name: vite

samples.description: Target is an alpha prerelease on a 0.x dep. Prerelease class wins over 0x-minor.
samples.name: schemars

samples.description: Target is older than current. Treated as patch-sensitive (issue) — downgrades are rarely safe and need audit.
samples.name: tokio

samples.description: Target equals current. No-op; routing is skip (nothing to upgrade).
samples.name: typescript

samples.description: Unparseable target version. Routes to issue (manual classification required). Never silently auto-PR.
samples.name: weird-pkg
